---
title: 'uv build'
---

The `xorq uv build` command builds an expression in an isolated, reproducible
environment using [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) tooling. It mirrors
`xorq build` but executes inside a uv-managed environment seeded from the script's
`pyproject.toml`, ensuring dependency fidelity.

## Usage

```bash
xorq uv build <script_path> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`script_path`** — Path to the Python script that defines the expression variable.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-e`, `--expr-name` | `expr` | Name of the expression variable in the Python script. |
| `--builds-dir` | `builds` | Directory for all generated artifacts. |
| `--cache-dir` | `$XORQ_CACHE_DIR` or `~/.cache/xorq` | Directory for parquet cache files. |
| `--project-path` | auto-discover | Explicit project root. Default searches upward from the script for `pyproject.toml`. |
| `--extra` | none | Optional dependency group to include in requirements. Repeatable. |
| `--all-extras` / `--no-all-extras` | `--all-extras` | Include all optional dependency groups. |

## Examples

```bash
# Build with auto-discovered project root and all extras
xorq uv build examples/penguins_example/expr.py -e expr --builds-dir builds

# Build pinning a specific project root
xorq uv build pipeline.py --project-path ./pipeline-project

# Include only specific extras (disables --all-extras)
xorq uv build pipeline.py --no-all-extras --extra ml --extra postgres
```

The build artifacts are written to the `--builds-dir` directory and the build path
is printed to stdout.
